title: [lora_l2] state machine: stop() sets aborted but returns 0 (success)
description:
State machine bug: lichen_lora_l2_stop() can set lora_state.aborted=1 (line 470) when the RX thread times out and is forcibly aborted, but the function still returns 0 (line 476).

How it manifests:
1. Caller calls lichen_lora_l2_stop()
2. RX thread doesn't exit in time, is forcibly aborted
3. aborted flag set to 1
4. stop() returns 0 (success)
5. Caller thinks stop succeeded cleanly
6. Caller tries to call start() again
7. start() returns -ECANCELED because aborted==1

The caller has no way to know from stop()'s return value that a forced abort occurred. They must separately call lichen_lora_l2_needs_reinit() to check.

Location: lora_l2.c:418-477

Recommendation: Return a distinct error code (e.g., -ECANCELED or -ETIMEDOUT) when forced abort occurs, so callers know immediately that reinit is required.
close_reason: Fixed: lora_l2.c now uses explicit state machine with lora_transition()
